texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Development of membranes for target application
texts.text: Development of membranes for target application

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The concept of Thin Film Composite Membrane (TFCM) allows for use of polymers produced in lab scale in pilot processes with modules containing 1 - 65 m 2 membrane area.
texts.text: The concept of Thin Film Composite Membrane (TFCM) allows for use of polymers produced in lab scale in pilot processes with modules containing 1 - 65 m 2 membrane area.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Design of membrane modules and membrane separation systems
texts.text: Design of membrane modules and membrane separation systems

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Flat sheet membrane geometry is suitable for large membrane area applications ( e.g. power plant flue gas separation) and for membrane reactors
texts.text: Flat sheet membrane geometry is suitable for large membrane area applications ( e.g. power plant flue gas separation) and for membrane reactors

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Mathematical models for description of processes in membrane and hybrid separation systems
texts.text: Mathematical models for description of processes in membrane and hybrid separation systems

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Aspen+ and Aspen Custom Modeler are used to model mass and energy flows in separation systems, which often include separation stages based on different principles
texts.text: Aspen+ and Aspen Custom Modeler are used to model mass and energy flows in separation systems, which often include separation stages based on different principles

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Analysis of the required properties of the separation material to be developed in terms of permeance and selectivity to meet the needs of the separation task
texts.text: Analysis of the required properties of the separation material to be developed in terms of permeance and selectivity to meet the needs of the separation task

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Usually, the separation process is designed based on the properties of the existing membrane. We propose to determine the properties required for the task, find a compromise between engineers and material scientists, and develop a new membrane.
texts.text: Usually, the separation process is designed based on the properties of the existing membrane. We propose to determine the properties required for the task, find a compromise between engineers and material scientists, and develop a new membrane.

texts.text: Porous support stable up to 250 ° C with properties reproducible in pilot scale production (200 m 2 )

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Very low selective material consumption: at selective layer thickness 100 nm material consumption 100 mg/m 2 example: Teflon® AF2400 72 000 €/kg → 7.2 €/m 2
texts.text: Very low selective material consumption: at selective layer thickness 100 nm material consumption 100 mg/m 2 example: Teflon® AF2400 72 000 €/kg → 7.2 €/m 2

texts.text: Mechanical strength is provided by relatively cheap nonwoven and porous layer

texts.text: S. Escorihuela, et al., Journal of CO2 Utilization, 2021, 101813

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gas separation with polymeric membranes is well established technology (H 2 /N 2 ; O 2 /N 2 ; CO 2 /CH4 ; C 2+ /N 2 …).
texts.text: Gas separation with polymeric membranes is well established technology (H 2 /N 2 ; O 2 /N 2 ; CO 2 /CH4 ; C 2+ /N 2 …).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Polymeric membranes have limited selectivity, it is no panacea, but can easily be combined with conventional separation methods, e.g. ad- or absorption, to obtain a highly pure product.
texts.text: Polymeric membranes have limited selectivity, it is no panacea, but can easily be combined with conventional separation methods, e.g. ad- or absorption, to obtain a highly pure product.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Flue gas (CO 2 /N 2 ) separation with conventional membranes is well understood process, several pilot units are currently in operation around the globe. (See Hereon and Holcim)
texts.text: Flue gas (CO 2 /N 2 ) separation with conventional membranes is well understood process, several pilot units are currently in operation around the globe. (See Hereon and Holcim)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The feed ( e.g. flue) gas should be properly treated before coming into contact with the membrane to ensure the intended service life of the unit.
texts.text: The feed ( e.g. flue) gas should be properly treated before coming into contact with the membrane to ensure the intended service life of the unit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The utilisation of CO 2 through chemical conversion into high value-added products with as low as possible energy consumption, better from intermittent sources, should make flue gas separation economically attractive.
texts.text: The utilisation of CO 2 through chemical conversion into high value-added products with as low as possible energy consumption, better from intermittent sources, should make flue gas separation economically attractive.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thermally stable polymeric membranes can be integrated into chemical reactor and help to shift the equilibrium resulting in higher product yield (CH 4 , CH 3OH…).
texts.text: Thermally stable polymeric membranes can be integrated into chemical reactor and help to shift the equilibrium resulting in higher product yield (CH 4 , CH 3OH…).
